title: Show interval due dates on reminders (1-day, 3-day, 7-day, 1-month, 3-month)

requirements.summary: Update DueReminders component to display due dates and new interval labels (1-day, 3-day, 7-day, 1-month, 3-month)
acceptanceCriteria: Each due reminder card shows the interval label (e.g., '7-day', '1-month') as a badge.
Each due reminder card shows a human-readable due date or relative time string (e.g., 'Due today', 'Overdue by 1 day', 'Due in 3 days').
The five intervals '1-day', '3-day', '7-day', '1-month', '3-month' are all represented correctly in the UI.
The old '30-day' label no longer appears anywhere in the frontend.

file_operations.description: Update the component to display human-readable due dates alongside each reminder's interval badge. Add logic to calculate and format relative time strings (e.g., 'Due today', 'Due in 2 days', 'Overdue by 1 day') based on the dueDate timestamp. Update interval badge labels to display the new five-interval set: '1-day', '3-day', '7-day', '1-month', '3-month'. Remove any references to the old '30-day' interval.

Create utility functions for formatting due dates and calculating relative time strings. Include a function to convert bigint nanosecond timestamps to Date objects, a function to generate human-readable relative time labels (e.g., 'Due today', 'Due in X days', 'Overdue by X days'), and a function to format dates as readable strings (e.g., 'Jan 15, 2025').

requirements.summary: Update TopicManager component to display all five intervals with scheduled due dates
acceptanceCriteria: Any place in the UI that lists intervals shows all five: 1-day, 3-day, 7-day, 1-month, 3-month.
Scheduled revision dates are displayed in a readable format (e.g., 'Jan 15, 2025') wherever intervals are shown.
No references to the old '30-day' interval remain in the frontend.

file_operations.description: Update the component to display all five reminder intervals (1-day, 3-day, 7-day, 1-month, 3-month) with their scheduled due dates for each topic. If the component currently shows a topic list, add a section or expandable detail view that renders all five intervals with formatted due date strings. Use the dateHelpers utility to format dates. Remove any references to the old '30-day' interval label.
